message:
feat(vendored/mux_client): tick 199 connect + send_request_only cx-first use timeout_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Two orphan-cx holes inside otherwise cx-threaded fns in mux_client.rs.
Both accept explicit `cx: &Cx` parameters AND thread cx into
checkpoint_mux_cx / verify_codec_version_with_cx / register_client_with_cx,
but the outer timeout wrapper on the socket I/O used ambient
`timeout(...)` imported from runtime_compat.

runtime_compat::timeout internally falls back to `Cx::current()`
thread-local lookup. When the mux client runs under a different
thread-local cx (or none) than the caller's explicit cx, the
timeout timer was bound to the wrong cx — the inner I/O future
observed the caller's cx via checkpoints, but the outer wrapper
watched a different one.

Tick 199 routes both outer wrappers through
`crate::runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx(cx, ...)`:

1. `connect_with_mode_with_cx` (L314): the socket connect timeout
   now binds to the caller's cx. Connect-handshake phase is
   therefore fully cx-threaded — checkpoint at entry, timeout
   bound to cx, then inner verify_codec + register_client both
   _with_cx.

2. `send_request_only_with_cx` (L1415): the PDU write_all timeout
   now binds to the caller's cx. A cancel fired mid-write now
   lands at the timeout boundary via the explicit cx path, not
   via ambient drop-cancel. Preserves the
   DirectMuxError::WriteTimeout error on legitimate timeouts.

Legacy connect_with_mode (L375) and send_request_only (L1353) are
left using ambient `timeout` — correct for those entry points
since no cx is threaded in.

Verification: 109 / 109 vendored::mux_client:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime,vendored via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target), including:
- list_panes_with_cx_read_timeout_when_server_stalls_after_handshake
- send_paste_with_cx_write_timeout_when_server_stops_reading_after_handshake

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
